reasoning: Both models agree the PKGBUILD downloads from official source with verified checksums, and the etcher script is a harmless launcher. No concerning filesystem/network operations were found in reviewed files.
coverage_issues: Both models failed to review the extracted .deb contents which contain the actual application binaries and resources
No review of potential embedded scripts in the Electron app (e.g., node_modules, preload scripts)
false_negatives: Potential risks in unverified binary blobs from the .deb file were not assessed
Electron app security concerns (e.g., native module exploits, insecure IPC) weren't evaluated
re_audit_focus: Extracted contents from data.tar.zst (especially balena-etcher binary)
learnings: File selection should prioritize executable binaries in binary packages over simple wrappers
Prompt should enforce deeper analysis of binary payloads when source isn't available
Add rule to detect/extract nested archives during file discovery
Improve model awareness of Electron-specific risks (ASAR analysis, nodeIntegration flags)
